mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in greater than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE rises above the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with a p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, which translates into higher earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns when FEDFUNDS is above 2.5 percent, or the signal reverses direction during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth indicates robust economic expansion and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 revisions show zero correlation with 5-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth surpassing 2.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in greater than 60 percent of periods when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 2.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when inflation is supply-driven.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market strength, supporting corporate profitability and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production combined with falling retail sales signals a synchronized demand and supply shock, forcing corporate earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in greater than 50 percent of instances when the trigger activates, or the signal fails to maintain a negative Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window.
